content: getComputedStyle always normalizes color to rgb()/rgba() regardless of how CSS declared it (hex, named, rgb), which is actually helpful for comparison. But pitfalls remain: transparent backgrounds can return the literal string 'transparent' instead of rgba(0,0,0,0); spacing/format must be parsed not string-compared; shorthand vs longhand properties (border vs border-color) resolve differently. Scoring logic must parse to numeric RGBA components before comparing, never string-equality.
